zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de la Pole於1430年2月出生在法國諾門迪時，她的父親William de la Pole II33歲，母親Malyne de Cay16歲。她於大約1455年與Thomas Stonor II在英國奧可福爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1494年2月28日在英國享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de la Pole于1430年2月出生在法国諾門迪时，她的父亲William de la Pole II33岁，母亲Malyne de Cay16岁。她于大约1455年与Thomas Stonor II在英国奧可福爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1494年2月28日在英国享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de la Pole was born in February 1430, in Normandy, France, her father, William de la Pole II, was 33 and her mother, Malyne de Cay, was 16. She married Thomas Stonor II about 1455, in Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 28 February 1494, in England, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de la Pole est née en février 1430, à Normandie, France, son père, William de la Pole II, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Malyne de Cay, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Stonor II environ 1455, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 février 1494, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de la Pole im Februar 1430 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de la Pole II 33 und ihre Mutter Malyne de Cay 16. Sie hat um 1455 in Oxfordshire, England Thomas Stonor II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. Februar 1494 im Alter von 64 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Pole è nata il nel febbraio 1430 nel Normandie, Francia, sua padre, William de la Pole II, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Malyne de Cay, aveva 16 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Stonor II circa 1455, nel Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 28 febbraio 1494, nel England, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de la Poleが1430年2月にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父William de la Pole IIは33歳，母Malyne de Cayは16歳であった。約1455年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでThomas Stonor IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1494年2月28日にイギリスで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de la Pole(이)가 1430년 2월 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de la Pole II(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Malyne de Cay(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1455년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 Thomas Stonor II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1494년 2월 28일 영국에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Pole nasceu em fevereiro de 1430, em Normandie, França, seu pai, William de la Pole II, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Malyne de Cay, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Stonor II aproximadamente 1455, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1494, em Inglaterra, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de la Pole родилась февраль 1430 г. в местности норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени William de la Pole II было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Malyne de Cay, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Stonor II около 1455 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 28 февраля 1494 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de la Pole nació en febrero de 1430, en Normandie, Francia, su papá, William de la Pole II, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Malyne de Cay, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Thomas Stonor II aproximadamente 1455, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1494, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años.
